prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for acrolein (8 atoms: O, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) using DFT at tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral); cation: charge +1, multiplicity 2; anion: charge −1, multiplicity 2. Geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file as-is (no re-optimization). Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). All integrity checks passed (trustworthy: true); sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f−) = 1.0000. No warnings reported.

Headline result — most electrophilic site: C4 (β-carbon), f⁺ = 0.64232102. Most nucleophilic site: O1 (carbonyl oxygen), f⁻ = 0.39388616.

Per-atom Fukui indices (f⁺ / f⁻ / f⁰ / dual descriptor):
  Atom 1 O:  f+ = 0.40136419, f− = 0.39388616, f0 = 0.39762517, Δf = +0.00747804
  Atom 2 C:  f+ = 0.02225511, f− = 0.00598844, f0 = 0.01412177, Δf = +0.01626667
  Atom 3 C:  f+ = 0.43404673, f− = 0.09140310, f0 = 0.26272491, Δf = +0.34264363
  Atom 4 C:  f+ = 0.64232102, f− = 0.08736982, f0 = 0.36484542, Δf = +0.55495120
  Atom 5 H:  f+ = −0.15725950, f− = 0.08596142, f0 = −0.03564904, Δf = −0.24322092
  Atom 6 H:  f+ = −0.20720290, f− = 0.18567960, f0 = −0.01076165, Δf = −0.39288251
  Atom 7 H:  f+ = −0.06211700, f− = 0.08448581, f0 = +0.01118441, Δf = −0.14660281
  Atom 8 H:  f+ = −0.07340765, f− = 0.06522566, f0 = −0.00409099, Δf = −0.13863330

The β-carbon (C4) is the dominant Michael-acceptor site (largest f⁺ and dual descriptor), consistent with acrolein's known 1,4-addition reactivity. The carbonyl oxygen (O1) is the most nucleophilic site.
